title: Nested strand bundle with ordered colors, even spacing, and clean 90-degree bends
summary: The image shows a grouped set of fiber strands routed as a clean nested bundle. The strands preserve standard fiber color-code order, keep consistent strand-to-strand spacing, and make simple orthogonal 90-degree turns without loopback, overlap, or crowding.
visually_observable_good_behavior: The colored strand routes remain nested as a coherent bundle through the horizontal run and the bend.
The visible strand colors follow the standard fiber strand color-code sequence from Blue through Aqua.
Adjacent strands maintain consistent spacing and do not visually merge into shared lanes.
Each strand makes a clean orthogonal 90-degree bend at the corner.
The route bundle avoids loopback shapes, same-lane overlap, and unnecessary crossing.

followed_requirements: Related fiber strands stay visually grouped as they move through the splice detail diagram.
Nested strands use adjacent routing lanes and move through the routing zone as an organized group.
Same-buffer-tube fibers route near each other and remain readable through bends.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: The strand paths remain side-by-side as a nested bundle and preserve a coherent group shape through the bend.

followed_requirements: Fiber strands display in standard fiber optic color-code order inside each buffer tube, fan out, strand group, and nested route group.
Nested routing should preserve strand order when assigning nearby lanes inside a buffer tube group.
Neighboring fiber strands inside the same buffer tube maintain the 24px pitch rhythm.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: The visible route order follows the standard strand color sequence and keeps that order through the nested bend.

followed_requirements: Individual fiber strand routes maintain the 24px grid pitch in all directions.
Spacing applies around bends, lane transitions, and between strand groups.
Strands remain visually separated and traceable without avoidable congestion.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: The strands are evenly spaced before, during, and after the corner, with no visible lane sharing or route merging.

followed_requirements: The canvas grid uses a 24px base pitch on both axes.
Fiber strands route on grid lines with approved bend points or transition points.
Strands inside the same buffer tube use adjacent or nearby lanes where possible.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: The routes appear aligned to a consistent grid pitch and bend together at clean grid intersections.

followed_requirements: All bends remain inside the routing zone.
Routes use available spacing instead of collapsing into a congested area.
Every strand remains traceable from the visible route segment through the bend.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: The crop shows clean center-zone-style routing with bends placed away from labels, fanout geometry, and reserved objects.

followed_requirements: Splice paths should use simple orthogonal route transitions.
Routes should avoid extra loopback bends when a clean bend is available.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: Each visible route turns once at the corner and does not overshoot, return, or create a loopback shape.

notes: This is a good reference example for nested grouped routing through a bend.
The image is especially useful as a positive contrast to same-lane overlap, broken spacing, and loopback bend examples.
The screenshot is a cropped route detail; it does not show the full CSV-derived cable, buffer tube, fan-out, or splice-dot context.
